mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Ceph and the need for sufficient resources to run effectively.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm client, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Rook to manage Ceph resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.2/deploy/cluster/examples/kubernetes/ceph/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Rook.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.2

```

> This command installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.2

steps.title: Create a Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a sample configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.2/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> This command deploys a sample Ceph cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> This command lists all pods in the rook-system namespace.
> This command checks the status of the Ceph cluster.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ceph data and resources managed by Rook.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> This command uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.2

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.2

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create pool'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ceph cluster is not healthy or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Ceph cluster using 'kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status' and ensure all nodes are healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are misconfigurations in the Ceph cluster or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rook-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'incomplete'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that not all Ceph components are running or healthy.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' to check the pod status and troubleshoot any failing pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitor pods are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the monitor pod status with 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' and ensure they are running.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance of Ceph cluster management, added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report a healthy cluster.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --create-namespace --version v1.19.2
kubectl -n rook-system exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that kubectl and helm are installed.
